recipe.title: Roasted Strawberries and Cream Popsicles

ingredients.name: strawberries
ingredients.note: hulled and halved; fresh ripe in-season preferred

ingredients.name: full-fat canned coconut milk
ingredients.note: vegan default; Greek yogurt, cream, or buttermilk possible substitutes

steps.text: Preheat oven to 375F; gather popsicle sticks, mold, and freezer space.
steps.visualCue: oven warming context only; not an opening beat
steps.runwayRisk: do not open on neutral prep lineup

steps.text: Arrange halved strawberries in an even layer on parchment-lined sheet pan; drizzle optional balsamic.
steps.visualCue: cut faces up, red flesh and seeds visible, even spacing
steps.runwayRisk: berry count and size drift if scale not locked

steps.text: Roast until soft and fragrant, about 20 minutes.
steps.visualCue: jammy collapse, darkened edges, pooled ruby syrup on parchment
steps.textureCue: bubbling juice, caramelized edges, steam wisps
steps.runwayRisk: iciness vs jammy gloss if roast state is weak

steps.text: Cool 10 minutes on pan, then transfer berries and juices to a bowl; cool to room temperature.
steps.visualCue: glossy syrup pool, spatula scrape ASMR
steps.textureCue: thick glossy red syrup dragging on parchment
steps.runwayRisk: hand fused with cloth if hot transfer staged wrong

steps.text: Pulse roasted strawberries with 1/4 cup maple syrup until chunky puree with small fruit bits.
steps.visualCue: pink chunky puree, visible seeds, not smooth sorbet
steps.runwayRisk: blender over-smooth if pulse duration not locked

steps.text: Whisk coconut milk with remaining 1/4 cup maple syrup, vanilla, and salt until silky.
steps.visualCue: thick white cream ribbons on whisk lift
steps.textureCue: silky ribbon trails, contrast with pink puree
steps.runwayRisk: coconut curdle or gray separation if over-whisked on camera

steps.text: Layer strawberry puree and coconut cream in popsicle molds (or blend combined for uniform pops).
steps.visualCue: alternating pink and white spoons into cavities, visible streaks
steps.runwayRisk: mold geometry drift; layer order collapse

steps.text: Freeze until set, about 4 hours; insert sticks after 30 minutes.
steps.visualCue: sticks upright in semi-set surface; frosty mold exterior hard cut
steps.textureCue: matte frost on mold, intact streaked pop interior implied
steps.runwayRisk: melting drip on hero if freeze state not held through outro prep

steps.text: Release pops by brief warm-water dip on mold; pull gently on sticks.
steps.visualCue: condensation rim, pop sliding free intact
steps.textureCue: clean release slide, frost on pop surface
steps.runwayRisk: destructive bite or drip must not appear in final pre-outro segment

assumptions: Hero format is layered/swirled pink-and-white bar pops, not fully blended uniform pink pops.
Standard silicone bar mold with 10 narrow cavities; each finished pop about 10-12 cm long and 2.5-3 cm wide at the stick end.
On-camera hero shows 3-4 intact pops plus mold context, not all 10 cavities filled in one wide shot.
Optional balsamic drizzle is included as a quick 0.5s accent, not a flavor headline.
Food processor action is represented with @Blender asset shape for continuity; no separate processor asset exists.

timing.cook: 30 min active (20 min roast + 10 min pan cool) plus processing

criticalTransformations: Fresh halved strawberries to jammy roasted berries with pooled ruby syrup
Roasted berries to chunky maple-sweetened puree with visible fruit bits
Separated coconut cream base whisked to silky white ribbons
Alternating pink puree and white cream layers marbling inside mold cavities
Liquid layered fill to fully frozen streaked pops with matte frost surface
Mold-bound pop to intact released pop via brief warm-water dip (no bite on hero)
visualTextureOpportunities: Hot roast juice beading on cut strawberry flesh
Jammy collapse and dark caramelized berry edges
Steam wisps over glossy syrup pool
Spatula scrape transferring berries and juices
Chunky pulse texture with seeds in pink puree
Maple stream into red puree and into white cream
Whisk ribbon trails in thick coconut cream
Spoon dollops creating pink-white marbled streaks in mold
Stick pressed into semi-set creamy surface
Frosty mold exterior and streaked frozen pop surface macro
Clean release slide with condensation on mold rim
possibleHooks: Macro hot strawberry juice bubble forming on a halved cut face before wider pan reveal
Jammy roast collapse with bubbling ruby syrup on parchment
Maple stream cutting into chunky pink puree
Pink puree and white cream spooned in alternating layers for visible marbling
Frosty streaked pop surface macro with intact cross-section color bands

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the on-camera hero emphasize fully blended uniform pink pops or the layered strawberry-cream swirl version from the recipe?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Layered assembly changes logical scenes, reference states, and segment layering prompts; we assumed layered/swirled unless you prefer blended.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Are you using standard narrow silicone bar molds (about 10-12 cm pops) or round ring-style molds?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Pop length and cross-section geometry affect scale locks and generated reference anchors.
